jobs.title: Senior Analyst – Pharma Analytics – OBU
jobs.description:
Job Title: Senior Analyst – Pharma Analytics – OBU
 Career Level: D1
 
 Introduction to role:
 Are you ready to turn complex healthcare data into decisions that accelerate access to life-changing medicines? Based in Chennai, you will join a high-performing analytics team that partners closely with commercial leaders to sharpen strategy, improve execution, and ultimately benefit patients.
 
 In this role, you will convert real-world data into clear, actionable insights that guide how we invest, prioritize, and grow across our brands. You will work hands-on with modern data platforms, advanced statistical methods, and visualization tools to influence critical choices at speed. Can you imagine leading analyses that shape the next wave of oncology launches and in-market performance?
 
 Accountabilities: 
 - Business-Driven Analytics: Engage with commercial stakeholders to translate priority questions into robust analytical plans that inform brand strategy, resource allocation, and market execution.
 - Advanced Modelling and Experimentation: Apply Design of Experiments, time series, regression, econometrics, Bayesian methods, predictive modelling, optimization, and simulation to generate credible, decision-ready recommendations.
 - Real-World Data Mastery: Ingest, engineer, and analyze large longitudinal datasets, including claims and EMR, to uncover patient, HCP, and market dynamics that drive measurable outcomes.
 - Scalable Data Engineering: Build and productionize efficient pipelines using PySpark and platforms like DataBricks to ensure reliable, timely, and reusable analytical assets.
 - Insight Storytelling: Create concise narratives and visuals in PowerBI, Tableau, MicroStrategy, Qlikview, or similar tools to communicate implications, trade-offs, and next-best actions.
 - Performance Measurement: Design and operationalize KPIs, test-and-learn frameworks, and dashboards that track impact, reduce uncertainty, and enable rapid course correction.
 - Quality, Governance, and Reproducibility: Document methods, code, and assumptions; enforce data quality checks; and standardize best practices for repeatable, auditable analyses.
 - Collaboration and Influence: Partner with cross-functional teams to align on hypotheses, stress-test findings, and drive adoption of insights in planning and field execution.
 - Continuous Improvement: Identify capability gaps, automate manual work, mentor peers, and contribute to a shared library of models, features, and templates that raise the bar across the team.
 - Delivery Excellence: Prioritize and manage multiple projects, meet timelines with clarity on risks and dependencies, and deliver outcomes that directly support business goals.
 
 Essential Skills/Experience: 
 - Quantitative Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university is required in one of the following or related fields: Engineering, Operations Research, Management Science, Economics, Statistics, Applied Math, Computer Science or Data Science. An advanced degree is preferred (Master’s, MBA or PhD).
 - 2 years of experience in Pharmaceutical / Biotech / Healthcare analytics or secondary data analysis.
 - 5 years of experience in application of advanced methods and statistical procedures on large and disparate datasets.
 - 4 years of recent experience and proficiency with PySpark, Python, R, SQL.
 - Working knowledge on platforms like DataBricks.
 - Working knowledge of data visualization – PowerBI, MicroStrategy, Tableau, Qlikview, D3js or similar tools is a plus.
 - Experience in MS Office products – Excel and PowerPoint skills required.
 - Proficiency in manipulating and extracting insights from large longitudinal data sources, such as Claims, EMR and other patient level data sets.
 - Experience with IQVIA data sets.
 - Ability to derive, summarize and communicate insights from analyses.
 - Organization and time management skills.
 - Expertise in managing and analyzing a range of large, secondary transactional databases is required.
 - Statistical analysis and modelling background.
 - ML a plus.
 - Statistical Analysis & Modelling: Design of Experiments, Time Series, Regression, Applied Econometrics and Bayesian methods.
 - Data Mining, Predictive Modelling & Machine Learning algorithms.
 - Optimization & Simulation.
 
 Desirable Skills/Experience: 
 - Strong leadership and interpersonal skills with demonstrated ability to work collaboratively with a significant number of business leaders and cross-functional business partners.
 - Strong communication and influencing skills with demonstrated ability to develop and effectively present succinct, compelling reviews of independently developed analyses infused with insight and business implications/actions to be considered.
 - Strategic and critical thinking with the ability to engage, build and maintain credibility with Commercial Leadership Team.
 - Strong organizational skills and time management; ability to manage diverse range of simultaneous projects.
 - Knowledge of AZ brand and Science (Oncology in particular).
 - Experience using Big Data is a plus. Exposure to SPARK is desirable.
 - Should have Excellent Analytical, Problem Solving ability. Should be able to grasp new concepts quickly.
 
 When we put unexpected teams in the same room, we unleash bold thinking with the power to inspire life-changing medicines. In-person working gives us the platform we need to connect, work at pace and challenge perceptions. That s why we work, on average, a minimum of three days per week from the office. But that doesn t mean we re not flexible. We balance the expectation of being in the office while respecting individual flexibility. Join us in our unique and ambitious world.
 
 Why AstraZeneca: 
 Here, data experts are embedded with decision-makers to shape the future of our medicines and the way we deliver them to patients. You will work with cutting-edge platforms and diverse real-world datasets, surrounded by curious, supportive peers who combine scientific rigor with entrepreneurial drive. We bring different disciplines together to spark bold ideas and make fast, well-informed choices, while valuing kindness alongside ambition. Your contributions will be visible, meaningful, and connected to a mission that improves lives, with room to grow your craft, your influence, and your impact.
 
 Call to Action:
 If you are ready to transform real-world data into decisive action for patients and the business, step forward and make your impact count!
 
 Date Posted 
14-Jul-2026 
 Closing Date 
 
 
 AstraZeneca embraces diversity and equality of opportunity.  We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry-leading skills.  We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be.  We welcome and consider applications to join our team from all qualified candidates, regardless of their characteristics.  We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements.

jobs.title: Business Process Analyst Manager
jobs.description:
A trusted partner for   millions   of customers, Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, hygiene, and infection prevention solutions and services that protect people and resources vital to life.   We deliver comprehensive science-based solutions, data-driven insights and world-class service to advance food safety, help maintain clean and safe environments, optimize energy use and implement smart water management strategies.   Building on a century of innovation, our 48,000 associates support millions of customers in more than 170 countries around the world.   
 This role is part of Ecolab s Global Analytics & Digital Solutions (ADS) organization supporting Global Supply Chain Planning. The Planning Business Process Analyst Manager leads a team responsible for delivering analytics, reporting, and digital solutions that enable data-driven decision-making across global planning functions. The role partners closely with Planning, Supply Chain, IT, and business process owners to transform business requirements into scalable analytical solutions that improve visibility, performance measurement, compliance monitoring, and operational effectiveness.   
 
 What you ll do:   
 Planning Analytics & Insights    
   
 Lead development and enhancement of planning analytics, dashboards, scorecards, and reporting solutions.    
 
 Partner with stakeholders and process owners to define business requirements, KPIs, and performance measurement capabilities.    
 
 Deliver analytics solutions that provide visibility into planning process performance, compliance, and business outcomes.    
 
 Translate complex business requirements into actionable reporting and self-service analytics solutions.    
 
 Drive adoption of analytics products through stakeholder engagement, training, and continuous improvement.   
 
 Ensure reporting and analytical solutions provide trusted, consistent, and actionable insights to planners and business leaders.   
 
   
 Data Strategy & Analytics Enablement    
   
 Collaborate with business and technical teams to establish scalable data solutions supporting planning analytics.    
 
 Partner with data owners to improve data quality, consistency, and usability.    
 
 Develop and maintain datasets, semantic models, and reporting structures that support planning decision-making.    
 
   
 Digital Transformation & Innovation    
   
 Lead initiatives utilizing Power BI, Snowflake,   Databricks,   Azure, SAP, and other enterprise analytics platforms.    
 
 Identify opportunities to automate manual reporting and streamline information delivery.    
 
 Support advanced analytics, predictive analytics, and emerging AI-enabled planning solutions.    
 
 Evaluate new technologies and analytical approaches that improve business performance and user experience.   
 
 Contribute to the long-term roadmap for planning analytics and digital enablement.   
 
   
 Project & Stakeholder Leadership    
   
 Collaborate across global teams to prioritize enhancements and align analytics   priorities   with business objectives.   
 
 Serve as a trusted advisor to planning leaders, process owners, and business stakeholders.    
 
 Ensure successful delivery of analytics products that drive measurable business value.   
 
   
 People Leadership    
   
 Lead, coach, and develop a team of business process analysts and analytics professionals.    
 
 Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, collaboration, and continuous learning.   
 
 Establish clear objectives, priorities, and development plans aligned with organizational goals .   
 
 Support talent development through mentoring, training, and succession planning.   
 
   
 What you bring:   
 Bachelor s degree in Supply Chain , Business, Information Systems, Engineering, Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field.   
 
 8 years of experience in supply chain analytics, business process analysis, business intelligence, planning systems, or related disciplines.   
 
 Knowledge of forecasting, inventory planning, supply planning, and related planning processes.   
 
 Advanced experience with Power BI, data modeling, dashboard development, and business reporting.   
 
 Strong SQL skills and experience working with large enterprise datasets.   
 
 Experience working with SAP and enterprise planning systems.   
 
 Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.   
 
 Excellent written and verbal communication skills.   
 
 Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.   
 
 Experience with Snowflake, Azure, Databricks,   Fivetran , or cloud-based analytics technologies   a plus .   
 
   
 What’s in it For You:   
 You will join a growth company offering a  competitive salary and benefits.   
 
 The ability to make an impact and  shape your career  with a company that is passionate about growth.   
 
 The support of an organization that  believes  it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.   
 
 Feel proud each day  to work for a company that provides clean water, safe food,   abundant energy and healthy environments.   
 
   
 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion     Ecolab is committed to fair and equal treatment of associates and applicants and furthering the principles of Equal Opportunity to Employment. Our goal is to fully utilize minority, female, and disabled individuals at all levels of the workforce. We will recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement based on individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, Ecolab will not discriminate against any associate or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, or disability.

jobs.title: Lead Business Process Analyst
jobs.description:
About the Role
 
 We are seeking a Lead Business Process Analyst to join our Analytics & Digital Solutions (ADS) with a strong focus on Operations Business Leadership (OBL) priorities. This role will involve working closely with Logistics, Planning, Commercial, Customer Service and in this role, you will lead initiatives that deliver scalable, action-oriented analytics solutions.
 You will partner closely OBL and data engineering teams to co-create high-quality analytics products, support design reviews, and enable structured deployments. As a functional analyst, you will also drive adoption, change management, and sustained utilization of logistics analytics solutions. This role supports global and regional logistics operations by delivering data-driven insights and analytics products that improve process efficiency, service performance, cost optimization, and continuous improvement, with solutions designed to scale across regions where applicable.
 
 What’s in it For You • The opportunity to take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, helping customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best • This is a fantastic opportunity to join a highly recognized global growth company offering competitive compensation and benefits in addition to career growth
 
 Key Responsibilities
 · Partner with OBL to translate OBL priorities into analytics-enabled process improvements
 · Facilitate problem definition, business framing, and outcome alignment with senior stakeholders
 · Collaborate with global and regional leaders to standardize and scale logistics analytics solutions
 · Act as a business process expert and contribute to knowledge management, documentation, and continuous improvement
 · Stay current on industry and analytics trends to identify opportunities for new technologies and practices
 · Act as the functional SME for logistics reporting and analytics tools, enabling faster insights and improved effectiveness
 · Ensure solutions are usable, embedded in operations, and delivering measurable impact
 · Support process efficiency, automation, and controls initiatives across logistics operations to improve operations
 · Identify process, service, and cost improvement opportunities, including AI-enabled use cases
 · Co-develop AI-ready analytical data models with data engineering and operations teams to enable action-oriented logistics insights
 · Design and deliver reporting for logistics KPIs, including transportation, service, cost, and execution performance
 · Ensure data availability, reliability, quality, validation, and reconciliation across all logistics analytics products
 · Lead design reviews, functional QA, and governance for dashboards, reports, and analytics solutions
 · Identify logistics and transportation process, service and cost improvement opportunities, including AI-enabled use cases
 · Ideate, prototype, and iterate solutions using analytical tools such as Power BI, python etc. balancing analytical rigor with usability
 · Plan and support analytics deployments, including documentation and delivery readiness
 · Own adoption strategy, change management, and value realization for analytics solutions
 · Stay current on operations, supply chain, and analytics trends to identify improvement opportunities
 · Ability to manage multiple, complex initiatives in a fast-paced environment
 · Mentor and develop associates to independently lead projects, strengthen business process expertise, and deliver measurable operational outcomes
 
 Minimum Qualifications
 · Bachelor’s degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, supply chain, or a related field
 · 10 years of experience in analytics, logistics, transportation, supply chain, or business process roles
 · Good SAP experience in Logistics function
 · Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with strong attention to detail
 · Proficiency in Power BI and working knowledge of SQL, Python, Azure and Snowflake
 · Strong communication, organization, and collaboration skills
 · Proven ability to drive change management and value realization through analytics solutions
 · Ability to manage multiple initiatives in a fast-paced environment
 · Experience working in Warehouse Management System (WMS) and Transportation Management System (TMS)
 · Working knowledge of ETL processes and data warehousing
 
 Preferred Qualifications
 · Experience working with SAP logistics, transportation and warehousing
 · Experience working with Transportation management systems such as Blue Yonder, Mercury Gate etc will be added advantage
 · Experience working with Warehouse management systems such as Körber etc will be added advantage
 · Advanced degree or certification in supply chain, logistics, analytics, or computer science such as ASCM CLTD etc
 · Team management experience will be an added advantage

jobs.title: Product Enablement & Support Manager - Business Intelligence
jobs.description:
At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, delivering critical insights and innovative solutions to help our customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments. With our worldwide reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to own your future and impact what matters. Are you ready to make an impact?

 The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

 Job Overview: 
 
 The Manager, Business Intelligence Product Enablement & Support is responsible for leading the global support and enablement operations for Ecolab’s Business Intelligence (BI) products. This role partners closely with Business Intelligence Demand, Product Development, and Data teams to ensure BI products are reliable, issues are resolved efficiently, and users are effectively enabled to leverage analytics to drive business outcomes.
 This role focuses on running the operations behind BI support, including monitoring, issue intake and triage, troubleshooting, stakeholder communications, and the establishment of a scalable, globally consistent BI support operating model.
 
 What You Will Do: 
 Responsibility for Business Intelligence platforms and products supporting global business activities across the enterprise

 Strong proficiency in Snowflake, including investigating data issues and understanding upstream and downstream dependencies

 Experience with ServiceNow and ITIL-based incident and problem management principles‑based incident and problem management principles

 Strong communication, interpersonal, and people leadership skills, with the ability to build effective, cohesive, and collaborative cross functional teams‑functional teams

jobs.title: Sr Project Engineer (Analytics)
jobs.description:
Position summary At Ecolab, we grow by delivering measurable outcomes—helping teams perform at their best while protecting what’s vital. This role builds and sustains an operational “cockpit” that connects engineering activity, operational performance, and basic financials into a clear, trusted set of KPIs and Power BI dashboards. You will translate data into insights, enabling leaders to focus on what matters most, simplify decision-making, and drive consistent execution.
 What you will do
 Collect and structure data: Identify, access, and consolidate data from engineering and business sources (e.g., ERP/finance extracts, timesheets, CMMS/EAM, project tools, spreadsheets), ensuring quality, traceability, and reliable refresh.
 Build scalable reporting models: Create and maintain clean data models and measures (Power Query, DAX) so KPI definitions are consistent, auditable, and reusable.
 Define KPIs that drive action: Partner with engineering, operations, and finance to define and document KPIs across activity levels, performance, and financials (targets, thresholds, and drivers), creating a clear KPI dictionary and “single source of truth.”
 Design the cockpit in Power BI: Build intuitive dashboards and scorecards (executive summary and operational drill-down) with trends, targets, variance to plan, and exception views that help leaders prioritize and remove noise.
 Enable a disciplined reporting cadence: Deliver weekly/monthly performance reporting and automated refresh schedules; provide clear commentary on key movements, exceptions, and required follow-ups.
 Collaborate as One Ecolab: Work cross-functionally to validate logic, resolve data gaps, and align the cockpit to the decisions leaders need to make.
 Strengthen governance and trust: Maintain documentation for KPI definitions, calculation logic, data lineage, and change control to reduce “number debates” and improve decision speed.
 Continuously improve and simplify: Identify opportunities to automate manual reporting, improve data capture, and enhance the cockpit as priorities evolve.
 Minimum qualifications
 Bachelor’s degree with 3 to 5 years of experience
 Candidates must be excellent in Power BI and Power Query, Good at MS SQL.
 Hands-on experience building dashboards in Power BI (Power Query, data modeling, DAX measures).
 Strong analytical problem-solving skills; ability to reconcile data, explain variances, and build confidence in the numbers.
 Advanced Excel capability; working knowledge of SQL (or equivalent) for querying and validation.
 Working understanding of engineering and/or operations performance concepts (e.g., throughput, backlog, cycle time, downtime, productivity, utilization) and comfort engaging with technical stakeholders.
 Ability to interpret basic financials (cost categories, budget vs. actuals, forecasts, simple P&L drivers) and connect financial outcomes to operational drivers.
 Clear communication skills—able to translate business questions into KPI definitions and simple, decision-ready visuals.
 Demonstrated ownership, attention to detail, and ability to deliver on a recurring reporting rhythm.
 Preferred qualifications
 Experience with modern data platforms (Snowflake data warehouse/lake concepts, ETL/ELT) and/or Azure data services.
 Power BI administration experience (workspaces, deployment, row-level security, governance).
 Exposure to ERP and finance systems; understanding of cost accounting concepts relevant to engineering (e.g., maintenance cost, project cost, capex/opex).
 Experience building executive scorecards and operational drill-down reporting that supports business reviews.
 Lean/Continuous Improvement, reliability, or performance management exposure.

jobs.title: Lead, HR Quality and Compliance
jobs.description:
Lead, HR Quality and Compliance
 Location: Hyderabad
 
 This is an office‑first role. Working together in person supports close, real‑time collaboration and team alignment, enabling us to deliver our best work. You will work primarily in the office, with the flexibility to work remotely for 40% of your time (two days per week)
 
 Intro summary of the role 
 This role is responsible for ensuring full statutory and regulatory compliance across HR, Payroll, and Talent Acquisition functions. The individual will proactively monitor legislative changes, implement necessary process updates, and partner with Legal, Finance, and HR Operations teams to manage compliance risk. The role also drives continuous improvement of payroll and TA processes while supporting audits, system enhancements, and key performance reporting. Strong stakeholder management and ownership of complex compliance issues are central to this position
 
 What you will get 
 An agile career and dynamic working culture.
 An inclusive and ethical workplace.
 Compensation programs that recognize high performance.
 A variety of benefits dependent on role and location.
 The full list of our global benefits can be found here: https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits .
 
 What you will do 
 To ensure compliance with all statutory requirements, financial regulations or any other instructions in regard to HR, Payroll and Talent Acquisition (where applicable).
 Identify and implement changes to ensure that the Talent Acquisition and Payroll service is compliant, to current legislation, financial regulations, company policy and Labour Law and Agreements (CBA) as applicable to role
 Proactively monitor compliance changes in respective countries and provide compliance updates to HR Operations & delivery teams, stakeholders and create/deliver communication plan to update impacted parties liaising with Lonza Legal team where required
 Whilst ensuring compliance and management of risk to always have the workforce experience at the heart of processes
 Ensure deadlines are met for statutory returns and payments where applicable.
 Continuous improvement of payroll related processes & controls including business continuity plans, approaches to delivering a modern payroll function, simplification of legacy approaches.
 Continuous improvement of talent acquisition processes & controls including business continuity plans, approaches to delivering a modern talent acquisition function, simplification of legacy approaches.
 Work closely with finance colleagues for monthly and period payroll-related activities including reconciliations, maintenance of control accounts as applicable within HR, payroll and Talent Acquisition.
 Leading audits that impact HR Operations and Delivery with support from wider team
 Manage ad-hoc projects.
 Take ownership of complex and technical issues and queries related to compliance and risk control.
 Advise the team and wider stakeholders on options and risks for a range of business decisions from a compliance and risk capacity perspective related to change.
 Cultivate relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
 To gather, analyse and provide the relevant details in support of performance indicators and targets as and when required  i.e. TA/WFA/payroll performance quality and accuracy
 To provide appropriate management information as and when required.
 To assist in the development and implementation of TA, HR & Pay systems and their longer-term development as required to support simplification of compliance and risk control.
 To ensure periodic statutory deductions from pay are properly reconciled and returns completed by the due date.
 Interacts and works closely with other HR Operations teams and the wider HR Community to deliver best in practice services to the Business and GPO.
 
 What we are looking for 
 3 years of experience in HR, Payroll, or Talent Acquisition compliance, with a strong understanding of statutory requirements, financial regulations, and Labour Law/CBA.
 Proven experience identifying and implementing compliance changes in line with evolving legislation and company policy.
 Experience ensuring statutory returns, payments, and periodic deductions are reconciled and submitted within deadlines.
 Strong process improvement mindset, with experience supporting continuous improvement, business continuity planning, and system simplification initiatives.
 Experience supporting or leading audits, with the ability to take ownership of complex, technical compliance and risk-related issues.
 Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to advise teams and partners on compliance and risk implications, while maintaining a workforce-first approach.
 
 About Lonza 
 At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30 sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
 
 Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
 
 Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

jobs.title: Senior Analyst - Agentforce
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary 
#LI-Onsite Location: Hyderabad, India We are looking for a Senior Analyst - Salesforce Agentforce to design, build, and support AI-powered agents on the Salesforce platform. The role will primarily focus on Agentforce agent configuration, prompt building, Salesforce development, and Apex-based customizations. The ideal candidate should have hands-on experience with Salesforce development, strong understanding of Apex, and practical exposure to building or configuring AI agents using Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein AI, and prompt engineering concepts.  
 Job Description 
 Key Responsibilities  
 Design, configure, and maintain Salesforce Agentforce agents  
 Configure agents using Agentforce Builder including topics, actions, instructions, and escalation paths  
 Understanding of different LLMs, tools calling and basic functions  
 Build and manage agent topics, actions, instructions, and guardrails  
 Build custom Agentforce actions using Salesforce Flow, Apex, and external APIs  
 Create and optimize prompts for agent responses and business workflows  
 Create and maintain prompts using Salesforce Prompt Builder  
 Develop custom Salesforce functionality using Apex, SOQL, and Lightning Web Components  
 Build and expose business logic through Apex classes, triggers, and REST APIs  
 Integrate Agentforce agents with Salesforce data, flows, Apex actions, and external systems  
 Ground agent responses using Salesforce data, knowledge articles, Data Cloud, or approved business sources  
 Configure and support Einstein AI features within Salesforce  
 Work with business teams to understand use cases and convert them into agent capabilities  
 Work with admins, developers, and business users to convert business processes into agent-driven workflows  
 Build integrations using REST APIs, Platform Events, Named Credentials, and External Services  
 Support testing, debugging, deployment, and monitoring of Salesforce and Agentforce solutions  
 Test agent responses for accuracy, relevance, compliance, and business alignment  
 Monitor agent performance, conversation quality, and error patterns  
 Implement secure agent access using Salesforce permissions, sharing rules, field-level security, and data access controls  
 Follow Salesforce security best practices including profiles, permission sets, sharing rules, and data access controls  
 Document agent configurations, prompts, Apex logic, and integration designs  
 
 Essential Requirements 
 4 years of experience in Salesforce development  
 Certifications Mandatory: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist  
 Experience in building and deploying enterprise grade Agentforce agents  
 Hands-on experience with Apex, SOQL and triggers  
 Experience with Lightning Web Components  
 Experience with Salesforce Flow, especially auto-launched flows used for automation and agent actions  
 Hands-on experience with Salesforce Agentforce or Einstein Copilot  
 Exposure to Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein Copilot, or Salesforce AI features  
 Experience with Salesforce Prompt Builder or prompt engineering concepts  
 Ability to configure Agentforce topics, actions, instructions, and grounding  
 Experience creating Invocable Apex methods and Apex actions for Flow or Agentforce  
 Experience integrating Salesforce with external systems using REST APIs  
 Experience with Named Credentials, connected apps, and secure API callouts  
 Good understanding of Salesforce security, including permission sets, sharing rules, and field-level security  
 Experience with deployment tools such as Salesforce CLI, change sets or DevOps pipelines  
 Strong debugging, problem-solving, and communication skills  
  
 Skills Desired 
Advertising Campaigns, Alteryx, Analytical Thinking, Brand Awareness, Business Networking, Curiosity, Digital Marketing, Marketing Communications, Marketing Plans, Marketing Strategy, Media Campaigns, Process Documentation, Statistical Analysis, Strategic Marketing

jobs.title: Sr. Associate, Content Ops Analytics
jobs.description:
Career Category 
Sales & Marketing Operations 
 Job Description 
 Position Overview 
 The Senior Associate, Content Data Analyst supports metadata tagging operations and content ecosystem reporting for Global Content Strategy. This role focuses on internal operations, helping ensure the accuracy, consistency, and performance of metadata tagging while enabling visibility into CCEL (cross channel engagement leads) operational performance through dashboards and reporting.
 The role requires strong analytical skills, hands‑on experience with reporting tools, and the ability to work with structured content data from AEM and related Adobe platforms. The analyst partners closely with India‑based content teams to support tagging quality, operational efficiency, and reporting reliability.
 
 Key Responsibilities 
 1. Metadata & Tagging Analytics 
 Extract, analyze, and validate metadata and tagging datasets from AEM and related systems.
 Identify patterns, inconsistencies, and gaps in tagging to support operational improvements.
 Support tagging audits through data validation, metric tracking, and reporting.
 Monitor and improve metadata accuracy, completeness, and consistency across assets.
 
 2. CCEL Operations Performance & Reporting 
 Develop and maintain dashboards to track CCEL operational performance, including throughput, turnaround time, and efficiency metrics.
 Provide regular reporting on tagging operations, highlighting bottlenecks and optimization opportunities.
 Support operational reviews by delivering accurate and timely data insights.
 
 3. Measurement, Data Quality & Visualization 
 Build and maintain dashboards using Power BI and develop queries using SQL/Python.
 Ensure structured, clean, and reliable datasets for reporting and analysis.
 Establish and monitor data quality checks and validation processes.
 Identify opportunities to automate reporting processes to improve accuracy, consistency, and efficiency.
 Document metric definitions and maintain consistency across reports.
 Support continuous improvement initiatives aligned with business objectives.
 Define and document standardized metric definitions and reporting methodologies.
 
 4. Tools & Systems 
 Strong proficiency in Power BI, Excel, and SQL.
 Working knowledge of Python or similar tools for data validation and automation.
 Experience with AEM and Adobe content ecosystem tools preferred.
 
 5. Stakeholder Collaboration 
 Partner with India-based content production and tagging teams for operational reporting needs.
 Collaborate with U.S. metadata and content strategy teams to ensure alignment in reporting.
 Communicate insights clearly to support decision-making and operational improvements.
 
 Qualifications & Skills 
 Required: 
 Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Analytics, Information Management, or related field.
 5-9 years of experience in data analytics, preferably in content or digital environments.
 Strong skills in Qlinksense, Power BI, SQL, Excel, and basic Python.
 Experience working with metadata, tagging frameworks, or content management systems.
 Strong attention to detail and data quality.
 Preferred: 
 Experience with AEM or Adobe Experience Cloud tools.
 Familiarity with content operations or marketing workflows.
 Ability to work in a fast-paced, operational environment with multiple stakeholders.
 
 
 
 
.

jobs.title: Sr. Associate, Content Ops Analytics
jobs.description:
Career Category 
Sales & Marketing Operations 
 Job Description 
 Position Overview 
 The Senior Associate, Content Data Analyst is responsible for supporting global content strategy by delivering scalable analytics, standardized metrics, and actionable insights across the content ecosystem. This role operates at a global level, partnering with U.S. and international stakeholders to define, measure, and optimize content performance.
 The analyst will drive reporting frameworks, develop global dashboards, and enable visibility into key content strategy metrics such as reuse, quality, cost efficiency, and adoption. This role requires a strong combination of analytics, stakeholder management, and strategic thinking.
 
 Key Responsibilities 
 1. Global Content Metrics & Strategy Enablement 
 Define and operationalize global content performance metrics (e.g., reuse, velocity, quality, cost, adoption).
 Translate business questions into scalable data models and reporting frameworks.
 Support strategic decision-making through data-driven insights and trend analysis.
 
 2. Content Ecosystem Reporting 
 Develop and maintain global dashboards that track the health of the content ecosystem.
 Standardize reporting across regions to ensure consistency and comparability of metrics.
 Identify trends, risks, and opportunities across content lifecycle and usage.
 
 3. Advanced Analytics, Insights & Visualization 
 Build advanced dashboards and analytical models using Power BI, SQL, and Python.
 Integrate data from AEM and other systems to provide unified reporting views.
 Generate insights that inform content strategy, optimization, and investment decisions.
 
 4. Data Governance & Quality Management 
 Establish and enforce data governance practices for content analytics and reporting.
 Define and document standardized metric definitions and reporting methodologies.
 Implement data quality monitoring frameworks to ensure accuracy and reliability.
 Drive continuous improvement in analytics capabilities and reporting maturity.
 
 5. Global Stakeholder Collaboration 
 Partner with U.S. Content Strategy teams to align on reporting priorities and strategic goals.
 Collaborate with global and regional teams to ensure adoption of standardized metrics.
 Present insights to leadership and support planning cycles with data-driven recommendations.
 
 Qualifications & Skills 
 Required: 
 Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Analytics, Information Management, or related field.
 5-9 years of experience in data analytics, preferably in digital content or marketing environments.
 Strong expertise in Power BI, SQL, and data modeling.
 Experience working with large, cross-functional datasets and global stakeholders.
 Ability to translate business needs into analytical frameworks and insights.
 Preferred: 
 Experience with AEM, Adobe Experience Cloud, or similar platforms.
 Exposure to content strategy, digital marketing analytics, or ecosystem measurement.
 Strong communication and storytelling skills for executive audiences.
 
 
 
.

jobs.title: Senior Analyst - Medical Analytics
jobs.description:
Job Title: Senior Analyst - Medical Analytics
 Career Level: D1
 Introduction to role:
 Are you ready to turn complex engagement and evidence data into decisions that accelerate real medical impact? Do you want your analysis to shape how resources are invested, how content lands with experts, and how patient benefit is demonstrated across diverse therapy areas?
 Based in Chennai, this role operates where performance analytics meets strategic intelligence. You will partner with Medical leaders and field teams to define what success looks like, measure what truly matters, and translate patterns into clear guidance that influences strategy. Your insights will help determine where to focus, which channels and messages perform, and how value is demonstrated across disease areas and tumor types.
 You will join a high-velocity, digitally savvy team that leans into accountability and moves from question to decision with pace. Expect to balance challenges with pragmatism—knowing when to dive deep and when to move forward with imperfect data—so that decisions can be made at the speed of the business.
 Accountabilities:
 Strategic Insight and Performance Measurement: Synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights that inform Medical planning, resource allocation, and engagement strategy across regions and therapeutic areas.
 Measurement Frameworks and metrics: Design and implement measurement frameworks that go beyond activity counts to capture engagement quality, content resonance, channel optimization, and strategic outcomes. Define and refine performance metrics aligned to Medical scorecards to drive the right behaviors.
 Impact Assessment: Quantify the value of Medical initiatives by connecting activities to outcomes, identifying performance patterns across territories and customer segments, and recommending actions that improve effectiveness.
 Requirements Translation and Analytics Alignment: Elicit and translate customer needs into analytical specifications that delivery teams can implement. Challenge unclear or unmeasurable requirements constructively and propose practical alternatives that move initiatives forward.
 Cross-Functional Delivery: Produce quarterly business reviews that surface trends, risks, and opportunities, with clear recommendations. Implement ad-hoc strategic analyses for urgent leadership questions, balancing speed with analytical integrity.
 Enterprise Collaboration: Partner with Commercial Analytics to align customer engagement measurement and share insights. Collaborate with Clinical Development on evidence generation metrics and clinical insights relevant to Medical strategy.
 Data and Advanced Analytics Partnership: Work with data and AI/ML teams to access enterprise data assets, leverage advanced capabilities, and identify automation opportunities that accelerate insight generation.
 Analytics Innovation and Automation: Evaluate emerging approaches and technologies for Medical use cases and champion pragmatic adoption when it adds real value. Explore workflow optimization and process automation to increase analytical efficiency.
 Data Quality and Integrity: Validate data sources, identify anomalies, and raise issues appropriately. Promote data standards that strengthen trust in metrics and insight.
 Essential Skills/Experience:
 Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in a quantitative field (Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Life Sciences, Business Analytics, Computer Science, or related field)
 6-8 years of analytical experience with demonstrated ability to translate business questions into structured analytical approaches
 Pharmaceutical or healthcare proven experience with exposure to Medical Affairs, Commercial operations, or Clinical development
 Proven track record delivering strategic insights that influenced business decisions and resource allocation
 Experience in matrix organizations where influence without authority and cross-functional collaboration are essential
 Desirable Skills/Experience:
 Medical Affairs domain knowledge including customer engagement models, content strategy, omnichannel approaches, or evidence generation
 Experience working across business units or therapeutic areas in global pharmaceutical organizations
 Background in performance measurement and impact assessment methodologies
 Requirements elicitation and collaborator management experience in analytical or business intelligence contexts
 Advanced data manipulation and analysis skills using modern analytical tools and platforms; ability to query databases and combine multiple data sources
 Proficiency with self-service BI for exploration, hypothesis testing, and insight discovery; statistical literacy to design robust measures and interpret results
 Process mapping and visualization skills to document workflows and analytical approaches
 Familiarity with AI/ML-assisted analysis for pattern recognition, automation, or predictive insights; programming or scripting (Python, R) is a plus
 Executive-ready communication and insight storytelling that connects data to strategic implications; strong written communication for business reviews and documentation
 Customer management across functions, levels and cultures; excellent English proficiency
 Self-starter with a pragmatic execution attitude who sees opportunities and drives projects forward with resilience and empathy
 When we put unexpected teams in the same room, we ignite bold thinking with the power to inspire life-changing medicines. In-person working gives us the platform we need to connect, work at pace and challenge perceptions. That s why we work, on average, a minimum of three days per week from the office. But that doesn t mean we re not flexible. We balance the expectation of being in the office while respecting individual flexibility. Join us in our unique and ambitious world.
 
 Why AstraZeneca: 
 Join a global, mission-led company where data and digital capabilities power every function—from freeing scientists to do more discovery to reimagining enterprise processes. You will work with curious, kind, and ambitious colleagues who put unexpected teams in the same room to spark bold thinking, using predictive analytics, smart automation, and modern data platforms to solve real problems that matter to patients. Here, you will be trusted to act, supported to learn, and encouraged to challenge how things have always been done—so your governance expertise translates directly into safer, faster impact at scale.
 If you are ready to turn thorough governance into a catalyst for responsible innovation, take the lead and help us set the standard for trustworthy data and AI today!
 
 Date Posted 
20-Jul-2026 
 Closing Date 
24-Jul-2026 
 
 AstraZeneca embraces diversity and equality of opportunity.  We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry-leading skills.  We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be.  We welcome and consider applications to join our team from all qualified candidates, regardless of their characteristics.  We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements.

jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Lead Data Engineering & Analytics
jobs.description:
Working with Us Challenging. Meaningful. Life-changing. Those aren’t words that are usually associated with a job. But working at Bristol Myers Squibb is anything but usual. Here, uniquely interesting work happens every day, in every department. From optimizing a production line to the latest breakthroughs in cell therapy, this is work that transforms the lives of patients, and the careers of those who do it. You’ll get the chance to grow and thrive through opportunities uncommon in scale and scope, alongside high-achieving teams. Take your career farther than you thought possible.
 
 Bristol Myers Squibb recognizes the importance of balance and flexibility in our work environment. We offer a wide variety of competitive benefits, services and programs that provide our employees with the resources to pursue their goals, both at work and in their personal lives. Read more: careers.bms.com/working-with-us .
 
 
 At Bristol Myers Squibb, we are inspired by a single vision – transforming patients’ lives through science. In oncology, hematology, immunology, and cardiovascular disease – and one of the most diverse and promising pipelines in the industry – each of our passionate colleagues contribute to innovations that drive meaningful change. We bring a human touch to every treatment we pioneer. Join us and make a difference.      
 Position Summary
 
 At BMS, digital innovation and Information Technology are central to our vision of transforming patients’ lives through science. To accelerate our ability to serve patients around the world, we must unleash the power of technology.  We are committed to being at the forefront of transforming the way medicine is made and delivered by harnessing the power of computer and data science, artificial intelligence, and other technologies to promote scientific discovery, faster decision making, and enhanced patient care. 
 
 If you want an exciting and rewarding career that is meaningful, consider joining our diverse team!
 As a Data Engineer based out of our BMS Hyderabad you are part of the Data Platform team along with supporting the larger Data Engineering community, that delivers data and analytics capabilities for Data Platforms and Data Engineering Community. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in data engineering, DataOps, cloud native services, and will be comfortable working with both structured and unstructured data.
 
 Key Responsibilities 
 Serves as the Subject Matter Expert on Data & Analytics Solutions.
 Solution Architect, with an ability to craft Enterprise level Data Engineering and Analytics Solutions.
 Databricks SME and Expert
 AWS cloud expert and architect.
 Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and communicate progress to the Data Leadership.
 The Data Engineer will be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the data products, evolution of the data products, and utilize the most suitable data architecture required for our organization s data needs.
 Accountable for delivering high quality, data products and analytic ready data solutions.
 Develop and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines for ingesting data from various sources into our data warehouse.
 Develop and maintain data models to support our reporting and analysis needs.
 Optimize data storage and retrieval to ensure efficient performance and scalability.
 Collaborate with data architects, data analysts and data scientists to understand their data needs and ensure that the data infrastructure supports their requirements.
 Ensure data quality and integrity through data validation and testing.
 Implement and maintain security protocols to protect sensitive data.
 Stay up-to-date with emerging trends and technologies in data engineering and analytics
 Closely partner with the Enterprise Data and Analytics Platform team, other functional data teams and Data Community lead to shape and adopt data and technology strategy.
 Accountable for evaluating Data enhancements and initiatives, assessing capacity and prioritization along with onshore and vendor teams.
 Knowledgeable in evolving trends in Data platforms and Product based implementation
 Manage and provide guidance for the data engineers supporting projects, enhancements, and break/fix efforts.
 Has end-to-end ownership mindset in driving initiatives through completion
 Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with minimal oversight
 Mentors and provide career guidance to other team members effectively to unlock full potential.
 Prior experience working in an Agile/Product based environment.
 Provides strategic feedback to vendors on service delivery and balances workload with vendor teams.
 Qualifications & Experience
 
 8 years of hands-on experience working on implementing and operating data capabilities and cutting-edge data solutions, preferably in a cloud environment.  Breadth of experience in technology capabilities that span the full life cycle of data management including data lakehouses, master/reference data management, data quality and analytics/AI ML is needed. 
 Databricks Expert and SME, along with DE experience on AWS cloud
 Experience with leveraging Gen-AI based automation
 Solution Architect, with an ability to craft Enterprise level Data Engineering and Analytics Solutions.
 Ability to lead a team of highly enthusiast Data Engineers
 AWS cloud expert and architect.
 Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and communicate progress to the Data Leadership.
 Ability to craft and architect data solutions, automation pipelines to productionize solutions.
 Hands-on experience developing and delivering data, ETL solutions with some of the technologies like AWS data services (Glue, Redshift, Athena, lakeformation, etc.). Cloudera Data Platform, Tableau labs is a plus.
 Create and maintain optimal data pipeline architecture, assemble large, complex data sets that meet functional / non-functional business requirements.
 Identify, design, and implement internal process improvements: automating manual processes, optimizing data delivery, re-designing infrastructure for greater scalability, etc.
 Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, PySpark, R, PyTorch, Pandas,  Scala etc.
 Experience with SQL and database technologies such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, Presto, etc.
 Experience with cloud-based data technologies such as AWS, Azure, or GCP (Preferably strong in AWS)
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
 Excellent communication and collaboration skills Functional knowledge or prior experience in Lifesciences Research and Development domain is a plus
 Experience and expertise in establishing agile and product-oriented teams that work effectively with teams in US and other global BMS site.  
 Initiates challenging opportunities that build strong capabilities for self and team
 Demonstrates a focus on improving processes, structures, and knowledge within the team. Leads in analyzing current states, deliver strong recommendations in understanding complexity in the environment, and the ability to execute to bring complex solutions to completion.​
 AWS Data Engineering/Analytics certification is a plus.
 
 If you come across a role that intrigues you but doesn’t perfectly line up with your resume, we encourage you to apply anyway. You could be one step away from work that will transform your life and career. 
 
 Uniquely Interesting Work, Life-changing Careers With a single vision as inspiring as “Transforming patients’ lives through science™ ”, every BMS employee plays an integral role in work that goes far beyond ordinary. Each of us is empowered to apply our individual talents and unique perspectives in a supportive culture, promoting global participation in clinical trials, while our shared values of passion, innovation, urgency, accountability, inclusion and integrity bring out the highest potential of each of our colleagues.
 
 On-site Protocol 
 BMS has an occupancy structure that determines where an employee is required to conduct their work. This structure includes site-essential, site-by-design, field-based and remote-by-design jobs. The occupancy type that you are assigned is determined by the nature and responsibilities of your role: 
 
 Site-essential roles require 100% of shifts onsite at your assigned facility. Site-by-design roles may be eligible for a hybrid work model with at least 50% onsite at your assigned facility. For these roles, onsite presence is considered an essential job function and is critical to collaboration, innovation, productivity, and a positive Company culture. For field-based and remote-by-design roles the ability to physically travel to visit customers, patients or business partners and to attend meetings on behalf of BMS as directed is an essential job function. 
 
 Supporting People with Disabilities 
 BMS is dedicated to ensuring that people with disabilities can excel through a transparent recruitment process, reasonable workplace accommodations/adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Applicants can request a reasonable workplace accommodation/adjustment prior to accepting a job offer. If you require reasonable accommodations/adjustments in completing this application, or in any part of the recruitment process, direct your inquiries to adastaffingsupport bms.com . Visit careers.bms.com/ eeo -accessibility to access our complete Equal Employment Opportunity statement. 
 
 Candidate Rights 
 BMS will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records, pursuant to applicable laws in your area. 
 
 If you live in or expect to work from Los Angeles County if hired for this position, please visit this page for important additional information:  https://careers.bms.com/california-residents/ 
 
 Data Protection 
 We will never request payments, financial information, or social security numbers during our application or recruitment process. Learn more about protecting yourself at https://careers.bms.com/fraud-protection .
 
 Any data processed in connection with role applications will be treated in accordance with applicable data privacy policies and regulations.
 
 If you believe that the job posting is missing information required by local law or incorrect in any way, please contact BMS at TAEnablement bms.com . Please provide the Job Title and Requisition number so we can review. Communications related to your application should not be sent to this email and you will not receive a response. Inquiries related to the status of your application should be directed to Chat with Ripley.

jobs.title: Senior Business Process Analyst
jobs.description:
Senior Business Process Analyst 
 Ecolab Business Solutions (EBS) 
 We are seeking a Business Process Analyst to join the Ecolab Business Solutions (EBS) as part of the Master Data Conversion team . This role plays a key part in delivering global SAP deployments , ensuring high‑quality, business‑ready Master Data across regions, business units, and complex data landscapes.
 The Senior Business Process Analyst operates with a high level of autonomy, leading Master Data conversion activities, partnering closely with business and functional stakeholders, and driving data readiness, quality, and governance throughout the deployment lifecycle.
 Key Responsibilities 
 Lead Master Data conversion activities for SAP deployments across multiple regions and business units
 Own end‑to‑end data transformation for one or more Master Data domains from legacy systems into SAP
 Assess data migration scope, complexity, and quality; define and coordinate data cleansing and remediation activities with business teams
 Develop, review, and optimize data transformation logic using ETL solutions to extract, transform, and load Master Data
 Provide clear visibility on data readiness, risks, and quality to project and business stakeholders throughout the deployment lifecycle
 Guide countries and regions through the end‑to‑end SAP data migration process, ensuring adherence to standards and governance
 Act as a senior point of contact for complex data and system issues, providing business and technical diagnostics and resolution support
 Contribute to the definition and evolution of data conversion standards, best practices, and methodologies
 Required Skills & Experience 
 Extensive experience working with SAP Master Data (ECC and/or S/4HANA)
 Strong hands‑on experience with SQL and ETL / data transformation tools
 Deep understanding of Master Data domains and corresponding business processes, cross‑domain dependencies, and data governance principles
 Proven ability to work independently and lead Master Data activities within complex, global programs
 Excellent communication and stakeholder‑management skills, with the ability to translate technical topics into business‑relevant outcomes
 Strong analytical, organizational, and problem‑solving skills
 
 Why Join Us? 
 Lead high‑impact, global SAP transformations
 Influence enterprise Master Data standards and governance
 Work at the intersection of business, process, and technology within a global organization

jobs.title: Principal RWE Scientific Research Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary 
The Principal Real-World Evidence (RWE) Research Analyst is responsible for the scientific and methodological aspects of all RWE projects as well as providing guidance for other members of the team.  
 Job Description 
 Key Responsibilities : 
 Produce analytic deliverables, including full study reports for RWE or observational database analyses projects.  
 Support project management by coordinating project activities and timelines as well as issue management.    Independently draft and edit documents such as high-level research proposals, protocols, and statistical analysis plans.  
 Develop project timelines together with the Real-World Evidence (RWE) Data Scientists.  Appropriately track communications with the customer as well as project related decisions taken.  
 Provide guidance to less experienced analysts on study design and analysis activities.  
 Conduct observational data analyses involving new creative approaches and oversee data management and statistical programming activities.  
 Provide guidance to conduct data quality reviews with detailed documentation.    Present research and analysis results to customers and stakeholders.  
 Produce analysis datasets, listings, tables, and figures for research projects, according to specifications, while maintaining documentation and complying with pre-defined project / study standards.  
 Perform in-depth research and quantitative and qualitative analysis independently. Provide guidance to Associate RWE Research Analysts and RWE Research Analysts.    Seek out opportunities for the development of new RWE services and new customers within Novartis.  
 Maintain familiarity with technical developments in RWE, epidemiological and data science fields.  Support in talent identification and advancement as well as hiring of new talent and team expansion strategies.  
 Develop department-level standards, tools, and templates.    Contributor to RWDA business development.  
 
 
 Essential Requirement : 
 
 Experience in the application of statistical methods to the analysis of observational data. 
 Technical proficiency in analytical and visualization tools and statistical programming languages such as SQL, SAS, R, R/shiny, Tableau, Python. 
 Deep knowledge of RWE data sources and standards such as CPRD, JMDC, Optum, Healthverity, PharMetrics, OMOP.  
 Expert in applied statistics. Extensive experience in the application of statistical methods for analysis of observational data including propensity scores, sensitivity analyses, etc. is a plus.  
 Good understanding of organizational processes. Extensive experience working cross-functionally with key internal stakeholders.  
 Open to experimentation and taking smart risks to support creative thinking that leads to practical solutions to healthcare and business challenges.  
 Holds a high standard on quality excellence. Continuously seeking to enhancing standards, technology through expansion of knowledge and training.  
 Support teamwork to swiftly and efficiently deliver innovative new products to patients and healthcare providers.  
 High ethical values and standards.  
 Able to speak out, challenge conventional thinking, and stand up for ideas.  
 Experienced in data visualization.  
 Excellent project management skills: can prioritize multiple tasks and goals to ensure timely completion.  
 Confident and competent when interacting with internal stakeholders.  
 Strong written/verbal communication skills. Highly effective at summarizing and presenting key considerations and evidence.   
 Strong team spirit.  
 Preferred Postgraduate qualification (Master’s or PhD) in a relevant field.  
 Preferred Publications or presentations in recognized RWE/biomedical journals/conferences highly desired.  
 
 
 Desirable Requirement : 
 
 Bachelor’s degree plus 8 years conducting research in the pharma industry, contract research organization, or academic institute; or experience in a closely related discipline within the pharma industry (e.g., clinical research, statistics, epidemiology, pricing).  
 
 Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion 
 We are committed to building an outstanding, inclusive work environment and diverse teams representative of the patients and communities we serve. 
   
 Accessibility and accommodation 
 Novartis is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to individuals with disabilities. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the recruitment process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please send an e-mail to diversityandincl.india novartis.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information. Please include the job requisition number in your message. 
  
 Skills Desired 
Building Constructions, Clinical Research, Clinical Trials, Drug Development, Hazard Identification (HAZID), Health Sciences, Immunology, Intensive Care UnIT (Icu) (Inactive), Internal Controls, Internal Medicine, Internet Protocol Suite, Job Description (Inactive), Medical Information, Organizing, Patient Care, Stakeholder Engagement, Utilization Management (Um) (Inactive)

jobs.title: Senior Analyst - Agentforce
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary 
We are looking for a Senior Analyst to design, develop, and maintain full-stack web applications on AWS while contributing to AI agent development on the Salesforce Agentforce platform. The primary focus is building Agentforce agent facing backend services, frontend interfaces, data pipelines, and API integrations that support marketing cloud intelligence use cases. The candidate should be comfortable using AI-assisted development tools and have basic familiarity with Salesforce development.  
 Job Description 
 Key Responsibilities 
 Location – Hyderabad #LI Hybrid 
 Build and maintain backend APIs and services using Python (Django preferred)   
 Develop responsive frontend interfaces using React or similar JavaScript frameworks   
 Design and manage data pipelines for ingestion, transformation, and analytics   
 Implement caching, session management, and real-time data patterns using Redis   
 Deploy and operate services on AWS (ECS/Fargate, Lambda, S3, RDS, SQS, CloudWatch)   
 Set up and maintain CI/CD pipelines, containerized deployments, and infrastructure as code   
 Write automated tests and maintain code quality standards   
 Use AI coding tools to accelerate development while ensuring correctness and security   
 Support the design and configuration of Agentforce agents including sub-agents, actions, and instructions   
 Assist in building integrations between Agentforce agents and enterprise systems using REST APIs and Platform Events   . Document systems, APIs, and agent configurations   
   
 Essential Requirements: 
 4 years of experience in full-stack application development. Strong proficiency in Python and JavaScript   . Experience with Django framework   
 Familiarity with React or similar frontend frameworks   . Hands-on experience with Redis for caching, queuing, or pub/sub patterns   
 Working knowledge of AWS core services (compute, storage, database, messaging, security)   
 Experience with relational databases (PostgreSQL preferred) and SQL    . Familiarity with Docker, Git, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure as code (Terraform or CDK)   
 Comfort using AI-assisted coding tools in day-to-day work   
 Experience with asynchronous job processing (Celery, SQS, Step Functions)   . Strong problem-solving, debugging, and communication skills   
 Exposure to Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein AI features, or prompt engineering concepts   
 Familiarity with LLM integration patterns (prompting, tool calling, RAG)  . 
 
 Desirable Requirements: 
 Observability and monitoring best practices (logging, tracing, alerting)   . Experience in regulated industries (pharma, healthcare)   
 Salesforce certifications (Platform Developer I/II, AI Associate, AI Specialist) are a plus  . 
 Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein AI, prompt engineering, Strong proficiency in Python,  AWS CDK and components, serverless or containerized deployment patterns, experience in deploying large scale applications on AWS 
 
 
 You’ll receive: You can find everything you need to know about our benefits and rewards in the Novartis Life Handbook. https://www.novartis.com/careers/benefits-rewards 
 Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:   Novartis is committed to building an outstanding, inclusive work environment and diverse teams representative of the patients and communities we serve.  
 
 Join our Novartis Network : If this role is not suitable to your experience or career goals but you wish to stay connected to hear more about Novartis and our career opportunities, join the Novartis Network here:  https://talentnetwork.novartis.com/network 
  
 Skills Desired 
Advertising Campaigns, Alteryx, Analytical Thinking, Brand Awareness, Business Networking, Curiosity, Digital Marketing, Marketing Communications, Marketing Plans, Marketing Strategy, Media Campaigns, Process Documentation, Statistical Analysis, Strategic Marketing

jobs.title: Senior Analyst - RWE Acceleration
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary 
The senior analyst, RWE acceleration, serves as the technical anchor for the RWD platform implementation and data integration workflows. This individual contributor role bridges strategy and execution- ensuring that platform investments are effectively operationalized within NOCC Hyderabad based study workflows. The analyst works in close partnership with the US data platform team and the innovation work stream to support tooling adoption, integration design, and workflow optimization across the Novartis RWD portfolio.

 Major Accountabilities 
 
 Support implementation and workflow integration of core RWD platforms that are either internal or external to Novartis
 Conduct user acceptance testing and quality checks for new platform features or tooling deployments
 Support evaluation of NLP tools from a technical integration and workflow feasibility perspective

 Partner with the RWD landscape and vendor evaluation analyst to ensure data source access is operationalized within platform environments
 Develop training materials and best practice guides for NOCC Hyderabad team members on platform and tooling usage
 Monitor platform performance, flag issues, and coordinate resolution with other team members

 Operates with significant autonomy on technical implementation tasks; expected to exercise independent judgment on workflow design and tooling evaluation . Partners with U.S. data platforms team

 Experience with data integration, ETL processes, or clinical/claims data pipelines

 Experience with pharmaceutical or life sciences data platforms are similar enterprise health data environments
 Experience with data from electronic medical records, registry databases, and external insurance claims databases for health outcomes research. Additional experience in epidemiology, market research, or clinical research is a plus.
 Experience in creating, reviewing, and maintaining codelists aligned with ICD-9, ICD-10, NDC, HCPCS, LOINC coding conventions for healthcare and real-world data projects
 Understanding of organizational processes, including experience working cross-functionally with key internal stakeholders.

 Confident and competent when interacting with internal stakeholders.
